symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly spreading unilateral hot tender erythema from a skin-break portal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unilateral spreading hot erythema with a portal of entry is characteristic of cellulitis.
Stasis dermatitis is chronic and bilateral.
Systemic features indicate significant infection/sepsis risk.
Absence of these features lowers (but does not fully exclude) necrotising fasciitis — remains a safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: Crepitus / dusky bullae / pain out of proportion on exam

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: These findings would mandate emergency surgical review; require in-person examination.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person assessment ± bloods (FBC, CRP, lactate, blood cultures)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess severity/sepsis and guide oral vs IV antibiotics.
To exclude DVT where the picture is atypical.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Surgical review / imaging if necrotising infection suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Necrotising fasciitis is a surgical emergency.
